(eight) Those in half-life are not dead, nor is the root slumbering under the snow (the symbol of both Christ and Dionysos: it looks dead but is only asleep, waiting to be awakened by the voice of spring). We are those slumbering roots. We are dormant. This is very much a confirmation of Aristotle's idea (mentioned in Dante) of three distinct human souls in each person, two of which are mortal (as I recall the vegetable & animal) and then one which is immortal, which is pure thought that delights in philosophy and contemplative wisdom, etc. Possibly, since what the daimon brought to me in anamnesis was memory of notable ideas and thoughts of Attic Greece, only this soul is retained by t

I had heard about the promise of the Comforter (the Paracletos) and believed it; I always expected it to save me when there was no hope for me. Thus I was able to prepare myself to receive it. It came to the door as I always believed. When I read the religious literature I see that few people have faith that the Paraclete will come to rescue them, let alone that is exists. The Mission people are making a dreadful mistake, not believing in things unseen. How can they believe that God has abandone

All the Protestant had inately (sp) was his conscience. Innately, I mean. Conscience -- individual and inner, and his own, not derived. But now! Whole categories of human thought innate, inner and individual, in the same way -- conscience elaborated or added to -- multiplex systems instead of the single system of conscience, which told us only what was right, not what was so. To the Catholic, it must have seemed as impossible that a person could have an actual again bite of inwit as it would see

This would create a Second Adam or New Man (in the old language, "free of sin," which is to say, "free of the sting of death," which means he --I-- can inherit eternal life).

The problem in the world is not evil it is death.

. . . In Aristotle the one sould of the 3 which is immortal is the one which seeks to know (seeks sophia).

"My divine children, whom I am preparing." (dream)

There is an implication in "St. Sophia will be reborn; she was not acceptable before” that I haven’t noticed. All other reali zations aside, this single sentence links Wisdom of The Book of Wisdom and Prov. 8 with Christ, as Christian thought has done, although it isn’t explicit —stated— as such in the Scriptures. This shows that this identification is correct. Thus this one single sentence contains even more information that I had thought, and I already got a great deal from it. And it is

But the truly remarkable thing is not what I know but how I came to know. In view of the way I came to know, I know with absolute certitude; there's that as well. To extericate me she had to infuse me as well as my environment; she had to get inside me, and so I came to know her secrets. One good thing, which I've noted before, is that the provider of the information is identified within the information; also her reason for giving it; also how she gives it, general y speaking (Aristotle
